title: Libinhania sphaerocephala
taxonomy: Libinhania sphaerocephala is classified within the family Asclepiadaceae, which is an obsolete classification. The plant is now reclassified within the family Apocynaceae, specifically within the subfamily Periplocoideae. Libinhania sphaerocephala is a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae. The species is native to Africa and is known for its unique, spherical-shaped inflorescences. The exact classification of Libinhania sphaerocephala may vary depending on the source.

intro: Libinhania sphaerocephala, also known as Phlomis sphaerocephala or Jerusalem Sage, is a perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Libinhania sphaerocephala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, particularly borneol, camphor, and linalool, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made with Libinhania sphaerocephala include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
